---
title: "Publications"
listing:
  - id: articles-list
    contents: articles/20*.qmd
    template: publications.ejs
    [...]
  - id: conferences-list
    contents: conferences/20*.qmd
    template: publications.ejs
---

**117.7** Research Interest Score || **45** Citations || **4** h-index

***

### 2024

## Publications

[10] **Hesketh-Best P.J.**, et al., Schroeder D.C. (2024) “Nationwide survey of honey bee viruses in the United States of America reveals shifts in dominant Deformed wing virus variants”. *Comms. Bio.* doi: https://doi.org/10.1038/s42003-024-07333-9

#### 

[9] **Hesketh-Best P.J**, et al., Schroeder D.C. (2024) “Sacbrood viruses and select Lake Sinai virus variants dominated *Apis mellifera* colonies symptomatic for European foulbrood”. *Microbiol. Spectr. ASM* doi: https://doi.org/10.1128/spectrum.00656-24

[8] Wallace B., et al., **Hesketh-Best P.J.**, Silveira C.B. (2024) “Globally distributed bacteriophage genomes reveal mechanisms of tripartite phage-bacteria-coral interactions” *ISME J.* doi: https://doi.org/10.1093/ismejo/wrae132

[7] Stiffler A., **Hesketh-Best P.J.**, et al., Silveira C.B. (2024) “Genomic and induction evidence for bacteriophage contributions to sargassum-bacteria symbioses” *BMC Microbiome* doi: https://doi.org/10.1186/s40168-024-01860-7 

[6] Varona N.S, **Hesketh-Best P.J.**, et al., Silveira C.B. (2024) ‘Host-specific viral predation network on coral reefs’ ISME Journal doi: https://doi.org/10.1093/ismejo/wrae240

[5] Nikulin S.L., **Hesketh-Best P.J.**, et al., Schroeder C.D. (2024) ‘A semi-automated and high-throughput approach for the detection of honey bee viruses in bee samples’. *PLoS ONE* doi: https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0297623 

### 2023

[4] **Hesketh-Best P.J.**, et al., Silveira C.B. (2023) ‘Viruses of sulfur oxidizing phototrophs encode genes for pigment, carbon, and sulfur metabolisms’. Communications Earth & Enviroment doi: https://doi.org/10.1038/s43247-023-00796-4

[3] **Hesketh-Best P.J.**, et al., Upton M. (2023) ‘Whole genomes of deep-sea sponge-associated bacteria exhibit high novel natural product potential’. *FEMS Microbe* doi: https://doi.org/10.1093/femsmc/xtad005

### 2021

[2] Koch M.J., **Hesketh-Best P.J.**, et al., Upton M. (2021) ‘Impact of growth media and pressure on the diversity and antimicrobial activity of isolates from two species of Hexactinellid Sponge’. *Microbiology Journal* doi: https://doi.org/10.1099/mic.0.001123

[1] **Hesketh-Best P.J.**, Mouritzen M.V., et al., Upton M. (2021) ‘*Galleria mellonella* exhibit a weight-dependent lethal median dose against Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus’. *Pathogens and Disease (FEMS)*. doi: https://doi.org/10.1093/femspd/ftab003a
